mcqs.question: Why is blockchain considered a distributed system?

options.text: It stores all data on a single central server
Multiple nodes maintain copies of the same ledger and coordinate through message passing without a central authority
It requires all nodes to be in the same physical location
It uses a master-slave architecture for data replication

mcqs.explanation: Blockchain is a distributed system because it consists of multiple independent nodes that each maintain a full copy of the ledger, communicate through peer-to-peer message passing, and reach consensus without any central coordinating authority.

mcqs.question: What is the key difference between a decentralized system and a distributed system?

options.text: There is no difference; the terms are interchangeable
A distributed system spreads computation across nodes, while a decentralized system specifically has no single point of control
Decentralized systems are always faster than distributed systems
Distributed systems cannot have more than 10 nodes

mcqs.explanation: While all decentralized systems are distributed, not all distributed systems are decentralized. A distributed system spreads processing across multiple nodes but may still have a central controller. A decentralized system specifically eliminates single points of control, as in blockchain.

mcqs.question: Which challenge of distributed systems does blockchain's consensus mechanism primarily address?

options.text: Network bandwidth optimization
Achieving agreement on a single version of truth among untrusted nodes
Reducing storage requirements
Increasing processing speed of individual nodes

mcqs.explanation: Consensus mechanisms in blockchain solve the fundamental distributed systems problem of getting multiple untrusted nodes to agree on the same state of the ledger, ensuring all participants have a consistent view of the data without relying on a trusted central authority.

mcqs.question: In a blockchain peer-to-peer network, what happens when a new block is created?

options.text: It is sent to a central server which distributes it to all nodes
It is propagated through the network using a gossip protocol where each node forwards it to its peers
Only the creator of the block stores it
It is stored in a cloud database accessible to all nodes

mcqs.explanation: Blockchain networks use gossip protocols for block propagation. When a node creates or receives a new valid block, it forwards it to its connected peers, who then forward it to their peers, until the block reaches all nodes in the network.

mcqs.question: What does fault tolerance mean in the context of blockchain as a distributed system?

options.text: The system crashes if even one node fails
The system continues to operate correctly even if some nodes fail or act maliciously
Faults are automatically fixed by the central administrator
The system prevents any faults from occurring

mcqs.explanation: Blockchain achieves Byzantine fault tolerance, meaning the network continues to function correctly and reach consensus even when some nodes crash, go offline, or deliberately send incorrect information.

mcqs.question: Which networking model does blockchain use for node communication?

options.text: Client-server model
Peer-to-peer (P2P) model
Hub-and-spoke model
Mainframe-terminal model

mcqs.explanation: Blockchain uses a peer-to-peer (P2P) networking model where every node is both a client and a server. Each node can send, receive, and validate transactions and blocks, with no hierarchy or central point of communication.

mcqs.question: What is the primary advantage of blockchain's data replication across all nodes compared to traditional distributed databases?

options.text: It uses less storage space
It eliminates the need for trust in any single entity and provides immutability
It processes transactions faster
It requires fewer network connections

mcqs.explanation: While traditional distributed databases replicate data for performance and availability, blockchain's full replication across all nodes creates a trustless system where no single entity can tamper with the data. The cryptographic linking of blocks ensures immutability that traditional replication does not provide.
